page: Microsoft Azure DS5_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor clocked at 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports instruction set extensions such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA. The instance is equipped with 56.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.5 GB of RAM per core, and includes 115 GB of local storage. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core performance is weak, as demonstrated by bottom-quartile results in stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression. However, the 16 dedicated physical cores elevate multi-core performance to average levels, as shown in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks remains in the average tier, with uncached read speeds reaching 101.38 GB/sec and write speeds at 47.39 GB/sec.

The hardware profile offers balanced resource density for workloads that benefit from multi-threaded execution without requiring high single-core clock speeds. It achieves average throughput in static web serving (17.56 GB/sec) and Redis SET operations (23.44 million operations/sec). Notably, LLM inference for text generation using a small 135M model performs in the top quartile at 276.01 tokens/sec, though larger 7B models drop to average speeds. This server is suitable for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded web serving, database operations, and moderate-scale text processing.
description: Microsoft Azure DS5_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv2 family, powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz. It features 56.0 GB of RAM (3.5 GB per core) and 115 GB of local storage, with no GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct tradeoff: single-core metrics in stress-ng and Geekbench are in the bottom quartile, whereas multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the average tier. Notably, the instance demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small language models. This resource profile is suited for multi-threaded applications, general-purpose web serving, and database operations that leverage parallel execution rather than high single-thread clock speeds.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS5_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 56 GB of RAM, and 115 GB of local storage, offering balanced multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DS5_v2 is a general-purpose VM with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 56 GB RAM, and 115 GB local storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated cores and balanced memory capacity for multi-threaded enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
56.0 GB of system memory and 115 GB of local storage
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Strong text generation speeds for small-scale language models
Suited for multi-threaded web serving and database operations
